text:
You’ve been wandering a bit around the village now.
What did you make of it?

options.label: It's calm, the farm still fell the best place to be though.
The people are interesting and some are hard to read.
It's nice but it all looks the same after a while.

text:
Calm tends to mean something different for everyone.
I guess you like the farm because it’s your own space. Any plans for it?
Efficiency first. Having things under control reassures most people.
You want them to see it. That's different from just having them over.
More space, or more of what's already there?

Hard to read usually means they're not performing anything.
Hard to read, or hard to place? There's a difference

options.label: Hard to place. They reminded me of something.
Both, maybe. I'm still figuring it out.
Hard to read. I couldn't tell what they wanted.

text: You will. It just takes a few conversations.
They usually don't want much. That's the thing.

Most people say that at first. Then they slow down.
What would make you slow down here?

options.label: Having something to tend to. The farm helps with that.
A good conversation. One that goes somewhere.
Noticing things i haven’t see before.

text: That's usually enough but I understand you’d want something more.
Well you're having one right now. Debatable, but still.
That might take a bit of time, but you seem to have plenty of it.
